channel.title: NPR Topics: Science
channel.description: The latest health and science news. Updates on medicine, healthy living, nutrition, drugs, diet, and advances in science and technology. Subscribe to the Health & Science podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: What a chimpanzee 'civil war' can teach us about how societies fall apart
item.description: A long-term study of the world's largest known community of chimpanzees has documented a rare event: what the researchers describe as the primate equivalent of a "civil war."
item.content:encoded:
A long-term study of the world's largest known community of chimpanzees has documented a rare event: what the researchers describe as the primate equivalent of a "civil war."

(Image credit: Aaron Sandel)
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: The real space science behind 'Project Hail Mary'
item.description: The science fiction blockbuster wowed audiences with its depiction of space travel and more. Here's what NASA staff and other scientists say about the basis for the amazing events of the film.
item.content:encoded:
The science fiction blockbuster wowed audiences with its depiction of space travel and more. Here's what NASA staff and other scientists say about the basis for the amazing events of the film.

(Image credit: Jonathan Olley)
item.dc:creator: Tara Haelle

item.title: After a whirlwind mission to the moon, astronauts are back home. Here's what's next
item.description: The Orion crew module containing the four Artemis II astronauts splashed down in the Pacific Ocean Friday evening.
item.content:encoded:
The Orion crew module containing the four Artemis II astronauts splashed down in the Pacific Ocean Friday evening.

(Image credit: Bill Ingalls)
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: WATCH: Artemis II astronauts splash down on Earth
item.description: The four astronauts aboard NASA's Artemis II mission splashed down on Earth, after a successful visit to the moon.
item.content:encoded: The four astronauts aboard NASA's Artemis II mission splashed down on Earth, after a successful visit to the moon.
item.dc:creator: Amina Khan

item.title: After more than 9 days in flight, NASA's Artemis II is set to return to Earth
item.description: The four astronauts on NASA's Artemis II lunar mission are set to return to Earth Friday evening. The crew is wrapping up a journey around the moon with a planned splashdown off San Diego, Calif.
item.content:encoded: The four astronauts on NASA's Artemis II lunar mission are set to return to Earth Friday evening. The crew is wrapping up a journey around the moon with a planned splashdown off San Diego, Calif.
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce

item.title: Artemis II astronauts have just one task ahead of them today: Return home
item.description: After swooping around the moon, viewing an eclipse, breaking an Apollo distance record and testing out a space toilet, NASA's Artemis II mission is about to return to Earth. Here's what the astronauts must face to make it safely home.
item.content:encoded: After swooping around the moon, viewing an eclipse, breaking an Apollo distance record and testing out a space toilet, NASA's Artemis II mission is about to return to Earth. Here's what the astronauts must face to make it safely home.
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: A new study helps explain how the brain creates mental images
item.description: A new study finds that looking at something and imagining it triggers the same exact process in the brain. It's also very similar to the process artificial intelligence uses to create an image.
item.content:encoded: A new study finds that looking at something and imagining it triggers the same exact process in the brain. It's also very similar to the process artificial intelligence uses to create an image.
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: We can't all be astronauts, but the Artemis II crew has fitness lessons for everyone
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts don't have a lot of space to exercise. That's why they've got the flywheel — a small device that can be used for strength and cardio workouts.
item.content:encoded:
The Artemis II astronauts don't have a lot of space to exercise. That's why they've got the flywheel — a small device that can be used for strength and cardio workouts.

(Image credit: NASA)
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: The Artemis II crew saw parts of the moon never seen before. Here's what they said
item.description: The astronauts on Artemis II observed parts of the moon humans had never seen before. Their findings provide a scientific baseline — and sense of wonder — for future missions.
item.content:encoded: The astronauts on Artemis II observed parts of the moon humans had never seen before. Their findings provide a scientific baseline — and sense of wonder — for future missions.
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: What the Artemis II mission might mean for the future of lunar research
item.description: NASA's Artemis II crew is on their way home. What will the mission mean for the future of lunar research? Astronomer and professor Jack Burns weighs in.
item.content:encoded: NASA's Artemis II crew is on their way home. What will the mission mean for the future of lunar research? Astronomer and professor Jack Burns weighs in.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson
